newsSince: No genuinely new AI-evidence-grade news since June 9. The LiquidCore product, the GF+Rittal full-scope polymer cooling loop for NETMOUNTAINS' 66kW high-density Velbert data center, and the direct-liquid-cooling white paper were all published Feb-Mar 2026, predating the prior case. Recent coverage is a re-surfacing of that earlier material.

sources.label: AI data centers: polymer piping in DLC white paper (gfps.com)

verdicts.rankRationale: The polymer-piping-into-direct-liquid-cooling pathway (LiquidCore, GF+Rittal high-density DC reference) is intact, but those disclosures are Feb-Mar 2026 and predate June 9, so they are not new evidence. -21% YTD keeps asymmetry attractive; flat price, nothing genuinely new this period. June 9 hold stands.
verdicts.bull: LiquidCore polymer chip-to-chiller cooling positions GF as a latent AI-datacenter DLC supplier.
verdicts.neutral: Real DLC product/reference exists but contribution is small vs the whole company; no new wins this window.
verdicts.bear: Industrial flow-solutions cyclical; AI-cooling revenue still immaterial; -21% YTD reflects core demand softness.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Hyperscale/colo adoption of polymer direct-liquid-cooling loops
verdicts.primaryRisk: Core industrial/automotive demand weakness; AI mix stays small
